title: Systems and methods for isoprene purification
abstract: Methods for the selective hydrogenation of acetylenic compounds in a product stream that includes isoprene. A method of selectively hydrogenating an acetylenic hydrocarbon in the presence of isoprene may include obtaining a hydrocarbon mixture comprising an acetylenic hydrocarbon, isoprene, and butadiene or cyclopentadiene, or both. If cyclopentadiene is present, the hydrocarbon mixture may comprise greater than 2 wt. % cyclopentadiene. The method may further include contacting the hydrocarbon mixture and hydrogen (H2) with a hydrogenation catalyst under reaction conditions that are more selective to the hydrogenation of the acetylenic hydrocarbon than the isoprene.
claims: Claims 1. A method of selectively hydrogenating an acetylenic hydrocarbon in the presence of isoprene, the method comprising: (a) obtaining a hydrocarbon mixture comprising an acetylenic hydrocarbon, isoprene, and butadiene or cyclopentadiene, or both, wherein, the hydrocarbon mixture comprises greater than 2 wt. % cyclopentadiene, if present; and (b) contacting the hydrocarbon mixture and hydrogen (H 2 ) with a hydrogenation catalyst under reaction conditions that are more selective to the hydrogenation of the acetylenic hydrocarbon than the isoprene. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the hydrogenation catalyst comprises nickel (Ni), palladium (Pd), or platinum (Pt), or combinations or alloys thereof. 3. The method of any of claims 1 or 2, wherein less than 10 % of the isoprene is hydrogenated. 4. The method of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein the acetylenic hydrocarbon is butyne, preferably 2-butyne. 5. The method of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein the reaction conditions include a temperature in a range of 30 to 50 Â°C. 6. The method of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein the reaction conditions include a pressure in a range of less than 10 bar(g), preferably 5 to 8 bar(g). 7. The method of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein the reaction conditions include a weight hourly space velocity (WHSV) in a range of 1 to 4 h "1 . 8. The method of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein the reaction conditions include a mol. % ratio of H 2 /butyne of 2 to 3. 9. The method of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein the amount of cyclopentadiene in the hydrocarbon mixture is 5 to 25 wt. %. 10. The method of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein the hydrocarbon mixture is a liquid. 11. The method of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein there is a complete hydrogenation of the acetylenic hydrocarbon. 12. The method of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein, the method does not include a dimerization reaction involving cyclopentadiene. 13. The method of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein the butadiene is added to a product stream from a pyrolysis or cracking process to form the hydrocarbon mixture. 14. The method of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein the cyclopentadiene is added to a product stream from a pyrolysis or cracking process to form the hydrocarbon mixture. 15. The method of any of claims 1 to 2, wherein the contacting occurs in a fixed-bed reactor. 16. A method of selectively hydrogenating butyne in the presence of isoprene, the method comprising: (a) obtaining a hydrocarbon mixture comprising butyne, isoprene, butadiene, and cyclopentadiene, wherein, the hydrocarbon mixture comprises 3 wt. % to 25 wt. % cyclopentadiene; and (b) contacting the hydrocarbon mixture and hydrogen (H 2 ) with a hydrogenation catalyst comprising nickel (Ni), palladium (Pd), or platinum (Pt), or combinations or alloys thereof, in a fixed bed reactor at a temperature of less than 80 Â°C, a pressure less than 10 bar(g), and a weight hour space velocity (WHSV) of less than 8 h "1 , under reaction conditions such that less than 7 % of the isoprene is hydrogenated, wherein the mol. % ratio of H 2 :butyne is less than 6: 1.
